originalTitle: Meta is adding ridiculous ‘rate limits’ and a soft paywall to its smart glasses
summary: Would you pay $20 a month for access to AI hardware you already own? That appears to be one of Meta's next bets. This week, it quietly announced that your glasses' Conversation Focus feature will soon be limited to three hours of use per month, unless you pay for a $19.99 Meta One Premium subscription. […]
content: Would you pay $20 a month for access to AI hardware you already own? That appears to be one of Meta’s next bets. This week, it quietly announced that your glasses’ Conversation Focus feature will soon be limited to three hours of use per month, unless you pay for a $19.99 Meta One Premium subscription. In a help article, the company insists that it won’t require a subscription to use your glasses, period; it’s merely erecting a “rate limit” for certain AI features. Even premium subscribers will only get 15 hours of Conversation Focus per month under that “rate limit,” it claims. Problem is, Meta’s rate limit is ridiculous. The Conversation Focus feature, which amplifies the voice of the person you’re speaking to so you can hear better in noisy environments, is not something that should plausibly be rate-limited, because it doesn’t use Meta’s servers. It runs on-device, using the chips inside the glasses that you’ve already purchased. I turned off my internet, and it kept working. [Image: Meta’s description of “rate limits.” https://platform.theverge.com/wp-content/uploads/sites/2/2026/06/chrome_n9OpnTvRVG.jpg?quality=90&strip=all] Here’s how the company introduced it last year: “[C]onversation focus uses your AI glasses’ open-ear speakers, beamforming technology, and real-time spatial processing to dynamically amplify the voice of the person you’re talking to.” Not only does it avoid Meta’s servers, but Conversation Focus doesn’t technically require an internet connection at all. I double-checked by turning off my phone’s Wi-Fi and cellular, turning on Airplane Mode, and I was still able to use Conversation Focus just fine by tapping a button on my phone. Does Meta have some secret licensing deal with another company that costs it money every time a person uses Conversation Focus? Failing that, the rate limit sounds utterly bogus. Meta is feeling some financial pressure trying to make AI happen, recently laying off around 10 percent of its entire workforce — around 8,000 people — to help offset its AI investment costs. It also recently managed to make three pairs of AI glasses $80 cheaper by nixing the Ray-Ban name. But perhaps ditching the branding isn’t the only way it plans to subsidize that move. At a time when hardware is getting increasingly expensive, I suppose this isn’t as controversial as Meta quietly beginning to embed a facial recognition upgrade for these glasses in millions of phones, code that it has since quietly removed. Still, I’m filing this under “Meta will ruin its smart glasses by being Meta.” We’ve asked if Meta can explain the move, and whether the company plans to put other on-device features behind a subscription. Meta didn’t immediately respond to a request for comment.
chineseTitle: Meta 正在为其智能眼镜添加荒谬的“速率限制”和软付费墙
chineseSummary: 您愿意每月支付 20 美元来使用您已经拥有的 AI 硬件吗？这似乎是 Meta 的下一个赌注之一。本周，它悄悄宣布，你的眼镜的对话焦点功能很快将被限制为每月使用三个小时，除非你支付 19.99 美元的 Meta One Premium 订阅费。 […]
chineseContent: 您愿意每月支付 20 美元来使用您已经拥有的 AI 硬件吗？这似乎是 Meta 的下一个赌注之一。本周，它悄悄宣布，你的眼镜的对话焦点功能很快将被限制为每月使用三个小时，除非你支付 19.99 美元的 Meta One Premium 订阅费。在一篇帮助文章中，该公司坚称使用眼镜不需要订阅。它只是为某些人工智能功能设置了“速率限制”。它声称，即使是高级订阅者，在该“速率限制”下，每月也只能获得 15 小时的 Conversation Focus。问题是，Meta 的速率限制很荒谬。对话焦点功能可以放大与您交谈的人的声音，以便您在嘈杂的环境中可以听得更清楚，这似乎不应该受到速率限制，因为它不使用 Meta 的服务器。它使用您已购买的眼镜内的芯片在设备上运行。我关掉了互联网，它仍然可以工作。 [图片：Meta 对“速率限制”的描述。 https://platform.theverge.com/wp-content/uploads/sites/2/2026/06/chrome_n9OpnTvRVG.jpg?quality=90&strip=all] 该公司去年是这样介绍的：“[C]对话焦点使用人工智能眼镜的开耳式扬声器、波束成形技术和实时空间处理来动态放大与你交谈的人的声音。”它不仅避开了 Meta 的服务器，而且从技术上来说 Conversation Focus 根本不需要互联网连接。我通过关闭手机的 Wi-Fi 和蜂窝网络、打开飞行模式进行了仔细检查，我仍然可以通过点击手机上的按钮来正常使用“对话焦点”。 Meta 是否与另一家公司有一些秘密许可协议，每次有人使用 Conversa 都会花费金钱
whyItMatters: 这条信息属于 AI 与科技趋势信号，适合用于观察工具选型、产品机会、开发者关注点和技术路线变化。
